tests.output_sample:
Analyze locally synced data with count, group-by, and summary operations.
Data must be synced first with the sync command.

Usage:
  hubspot-pp-cli analytics [flags]

Examples:
  # Count records by type
  hubspot-pp-cli analytics --type messages

  # Group by a field
  hubspot-pp-cli analytics --type messages --group-by author_id

  # Top 10 most frequent values
  hubspot-pp-cli analytics --type messages --group-by channel_id --limit 10 --json

Flags:
      --db string         Database path
      --group-by string   Field to group by
  -h, --help              help for analytics
      --limit int         Max groups to show (default 25)
      --type string       Resource type to analyze

Global Flags:
      --agent                   Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --allow-partial-failure   Downgrade response-body partial-failure (e.g. partialFailureError) to a warning instead of a non-zero exit
      --compact                 Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage
      --config string           Config file path
      --csv                     Output as CSV (table and array responses)
      --data-source string      Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default "auto")
      --deliver string          Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run                 Show request without sending
      --human-friendly          Enable colored output and rich formatting
      --idempotent              Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op
      --ignore-missing          Treat missing delete targets as a successful no-op
      --json                    Output as JSON
      --max-age duration        Maximum acceptable age of local-store data before a stderr hint suggests sync; 0 disables (default 30m0s)
      --no-cache                Bypass response cache
      --no-color                Disable colored output
      --no-input                Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                   Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string          Apply values from a saved profile (see 'hubspot-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                   Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float        Max requests per second (0 to disable)
      --select string           Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)
      --timeout duration        Request timeout (default 1m0s)
      --yes                     Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)

messages: 0 records
hint: local store has not been synced yet. Run 'hubspot-pp-cli sync' before trusting local results.

Browse and call any API endpoint using the raw interface names.

The friendly top-level commands cover the most common operations.
This command provides access to ALL endpoints for power users and
agents that need full API coverage.

Run 'api' with no arguments to list all interfaces.
Run 'api <interface>' to see that interface's methods.

Usage:
  hubspot-pp-cli api [interface] [flags]

Examples:
  # List all available interfaces
  hubspot-pp-cli api

  # Show methods for a specific interface
  hubspot-pp-cli api <interface-name>

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for api

Global Flags:

      --dry-run                 Show request without sending
      --human-friendly          Enable colored output and rich formatting

      --ignore-missing          Treat missing delete targets as a successful no-op
      --json                    Output as JSON
      --max-age duration        Maximum acceptable age of local-store data before a stderr hint suggests sync; 0 disables (default 30m0s)
      --no-cache                Bypass response cache
      --no-color                Disable colored output
      --no-input                Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                   Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string          Apply values from a saved profile (see 'hubspot-pp-cli profile list')

tests.output_sample:
Read a CSV of contact updates and validate each row against HubSpot's
property schema (types, picklists) before any mutation. Use --dry-run to
inspect the validation report; without --dry-run, dispatches the changes via
POST /crm/v3/objects/contacts/batch/update in 100-record chunks.

The CSV header row names the columns. By default each column maps to a
property of the same name (lowercased). Use --map to override:
  --map "FirstName=firstname,SfdcOpp=sfdc_opportunity_id".

Rows are identified by an 'id' or 'email' column (id wins when both present).

Usage:
  hubspot-pp-cli contacts bulk-update [flags]

Examples:
  # Validate only
  hubspot-pp-cli contacts bulk-update --from-csv updates.csv --dry-run

  # Apply
  hubspot-pp-cli contacts bulk-update --from-csv updates.csv --map "FirstName=firstname"

Flags:
      --db string         Database path
      --from-csv string   Path to the CSV file with the updates
  -h, --help              help for bulk-update
      --map string        Comma-separated column-to-property remapping, e.g. "FirstName=firstname"

Global Flags:
      --agent                   Set all agent-friendly defaults (--json --compact --no-input --no-color --yes)
      --allow-partial-failure   Downgrade response-body partial-failure (e.g. partialFailureError) to a warning instead of a non-zero exit

      --deliver string          Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>
      --dry-run                 Show request without sending

      --idempotent              Treat already-existing create results as a successful no-op

      --json                    Output as JSON
      --max-age duration        Maximum acceptable age of local-store data before a stderr hint suggests sync; 0 disables (default 30m0s)
      --no-cache                Bypass response cache
      --no-color                Disable colored output

      --profile string          Apply values from a saved profile (see 'hubspot-pp-cli profile list')

      --select string           Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)

tests.output_sample:
Score = signal_score * 2 + log(amount) * 1.5 + stage_probability * 3 + recency_score * 2. Reads from the local SQLite mirror.

Usage:
  hubspot-pp-cli deals top [flags]
